free_text: Crosschecking of alpha particle monitor reactions up
to 50 MeV
Natural Al (24.1um), Ti (10.9um), Cu (7.61um)
high purity metallic foils
One stack
consisting of Al Ti and Cu foils.
Irradiation was done in a Faraday-cup like targetholder
with alpha beam of 51.25MeV, 110nA for 1 hour.
Beam intensity was monitored and registered in every
minutes. Recoiled reaction products were collected
with thin Al catcher foil and the activity was measured
together with the target foil in front.
No chemical separation was applied.
Several series of spectra were recorded to
be able to follow the decay.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT online data library.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-1.0MeV in the last
target foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included. Overall uncertainty were about 10%.
surface density of target nuclei(1%),
incident charged particle flux (3%),
nuclear decay and branching data(3%)
detector efficiency (3-6%)
statistical and peak fitting (0.3-7%)
